title: Using `--pid=host` in rootless mode doesn't clean up backgrounded child processes (including conmon)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Using `--pid=host` in rootless mode means any background processes and the conmon process itself will continue running even after the podman container is stopped/deleted.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Start a rootless container like this `podman run -it --pid=host ubuntu bash`

2. Start a background process: `sleep 86400 &`

3. Exit the container

4. Look at your process tree and see that conmon and the background process are still running.

**Describe the results you received:**

podman/conmon are not cleaning up backgrounded processes.

**Describe the results you expected:**

podman/conmon should clean up backgrounded process started within the container regardless of whether `--pid=host` is used or not.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Wed Dec 31 18:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

I have also tried a source built podman with the same results:

```
Version:      4.0.0-dev
API Version:  4.0.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.13.8
Git Commit:   1de96f2c4c390bae29c303e2390da9e87a993d3f
Built:        Fri Oct  1 17:18:12 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  os: linux
  remoteSocket:

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

comments.body:
Did you actually stop the container with `podman stop`? Or just do a `exit` from bash in the container?

Given that there's no PID namespace, the container could technically still be construed as running even if PID 1 is stopped. Does `podman ps` show the container as running, or stopped?
@mheon Result is same after `exit`, `podman stop`, `podman rm`. `podman ps` shows the container is stopped after `exit`. The processes are never cleaned up and have to be manually killed.

with cgroup v1, rootless containers cannot use cgroups, so when there is no PID namespace there is really nothing tracking the container.

I don't think there is anything we can do except not allowing `--pid=host` with rootless on cgroup v1.

If using a PID namespace is not an option, I suggest using cgroup v2

The process hierarchy still appears beneath `conmon` in this case.

Here is what it looks like (using `ps auxwf`) after I start the backgrounded sleep process while the container is running:

```
joelm     988145  0.0  0.0  94728  2028 ?        Ssl  13:41   0:00  \_ /usr/libexec/podman/conmon --api-version 1 -c 2b3e78783...
joelm     988148  0.0  0.0   4632  4004 pts/0    Ss+  13:41   0:00      \_ bash
joelm     988152  0.0  0.0   2516   592 pts/0    S    13:41   0:00          \_ sleep 86400
```

And here is what it looks like after the container has exited/stopped:

```
joelm     988145  0.0  0.0  94728  2028 ?        Ssl  13:41   0:00  \_ /usr/libexec/podman/conmon --api-version 1 -c 2b3e78783...
joelm     988152  0.0  0.0   2516   592 ?        S    13:41   0:00      \_ sleep 86400
```

I understand that without cgroups you wouldn't be able to track processes that go through the full dissociation / daemonization process, but for this common case (backgrounded but still associated with a parent process) surely `podman`/`conmon` could do a better job of killing/reaping child processes that are still parented to `conmon`?
